changes.value: Window menu separator always appended even when no "window" manifest entries exist

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-kanban-app/src/menu.rs:127

The Window menu block appends a separator unconditionally inside the `if let Some(items)` branch:

```rust
if let Some(items) = menus.get("window") {
    // ... append items ...
    window_menu.append(&PredefinedMenuItem::separator(app)?)?;  // always runs
}
window_menu.append(&PredefinedMenuItem::minimize(app, None)?)?;
```

If the manifest has window items but the last item is already in a group that would naturally end with a separator, this produces a double separator. More importantly, if the frontend sends an empty window group, the separator is appended between nothing and Minimize.

This is the same separator-management style used in the existing File/App menus, which track `last_group` to insert separators only between groups. The window menu hardcodes the separator without that guard.

Suggestion: Wrap the separator in a guard, or only append it when `items` is non-empty (it always will be here, but the pattern is inconsistent)."
\ No newline at end of file
